title: Upon Spokes.
content:
Spokes, when he sees a roasted pig, he swears
    Nothing he loves on't but the chaps and ears:
    But carve to him the fat flanks, and he shall
    Rid these, and those, and part by part eat all.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Upon Spokes."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
